methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
IQM Quantum Computers is a pre-IPO quantum computing company founded by Jan Goetz that has raised €600M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 known comparable companies: 3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation is not applicable here. The quantum computing peer set, while containing 7 same-sector companies, lacks sufficient clustering at IQM's stage and valuation tier. Specifically, no peers meet the threshold of three or more datapoints when filtered for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier criteria simultaneously. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity in quantum technology approaches, maturity, and capital structures across the comp set.

The absence of a reliable median multiple method leaves IQM's valuation dependent on case-specific factors: capital deployed (€600M+ raised), technical differentiation within quantum architectures, and customer pipeline maturity relative to the listed and private peer base. Investors should weight IQM against individual comparable transactions and recent fundraising rounds rather than derived multiples. Key uncertainties include the stability of quantum technology roadmaps and near-term commercialization timelines across the sector, both of which drive wide valuation dispersion in this emerging domain.
